title: fagraea berteroana
intro: Fagraea berteroana, also known as the 'Ma'o hau hele' in Hawaiian, is a rare, endangered, and flowering plant native to Hawaii. It is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Gentianaceae and is known for its fragrant, white, and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Fagraea berteroana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent.
